chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/318)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/318)
text: تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِي الثَّوْبِ الْمَصْبُوغِ الْمُشْبَعِ الْمُفْدَمِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Hammad Bin Usman,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Salāt is dislike in the dyed clothes in vibrant colours’.
en.sarwar: 22. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is undesirable to perform Salah (prayer) in clothes dyed with sharply saturated colors.”’
